jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with typical Homer mishap
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated character moment but routine by this point in the series

jokes.text: Marge, stop this crazy thing!
jokes.setup: Homer apparently stuck in some mechanical situation
jokes.punchline: Reference to The Jetsons' George Jetson catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that fits Homer's character and situation, solid but not exceptional

jokes.text: Well, they put us on the Waiting to Exhale waiting list... but they said don't hold your breath.
jokes.setup: Family trying to rent the movie Waiting to Exhale
jokes.punchline: Pun combining the movie title with the breathing idiom
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed pun that works on multiple levels, good economy of language

jokes.text: A Clint Eastwood, Lee Marvin shoot-'em-up western! [Both Groaning]
jokes.setup: Homer claims he got something 'exactly along those lines' as the requested romantic movies
jokes.punchline: Complete genre opposite + family's disappointed groaning reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness, strong character moment with good misdirection structure

jokes.text: Joshua Logan's Paint Your Wagon. With blood, I bet. [Both Laughing]
jokes.setup: Homer promises 'bloody mayhem and unholy carnage'
jokes.punchline: It's actually a musical about painting, kids laugh at the disconnect
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that sets up the entire episode's premise, well-earned surprise

jokes.text: [Shrieks] They're singing! They're singing, Marge. Why aren't they killing each other?
jokes.setup: Homer expected a violent western
jokes.punchline: His horror at discovering it's a musical
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction, treats singing like a disease or disaster, great character integration

jokes.text: Yeah, their guns are right there. Wait, wait, wait. Here comes Lee Marvin. Thank God. He's always drunk and violent.
jokes.setup: Homer's continued confusion about the peaceful musical
jokes.punchline: His faith that Lee Marvin will restore expected violence
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency, Homer's desperate hope for violence, solid observational humor

jokes.text: You missed a spot. Well, grab a brush and join in.
jokes.setup: Confrontational setup suggesting conflict
jokes.punchline: Instead of violence, Lee Marvin just wants to help paint
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectations of western confrontation with wholesome cooperation

jokes.text: Mmm. Who knew that Lee Marvin could do such marvelous splits?
jokes.setup: Lee Marvin, tough guy actor, in a musical number
jokes.punchline: Marge appreciating his unexpected dancing skills
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between tough guy image and musical theater, good visual comedy

jokes.text: Oh, why did they have to screw up a perfectly serviceable wagon story... with all that fruity singing?
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting the musical format
jokes.punchline: 'Fruity singing' dismissal of musicals
jokes.explanation: Homer's crude dismissal of musical theater, good character voice

jokes.text: Singing is the lowest form of communication. Homer, you sing all the time. No, I don't. I hate to rhyme.
jokes.setup: Homer declares singing the lowest form of communication
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by rhyming, then launches into song
jokes.explanation: Perfect hypocrisy moment, Homer immediately does what he claims to hate, excellent self-contradiction

jokes.text: Hello! Human Fly here. Come on! I stayed up all night dyeing my underwear.
jokes.setup: Minor character trying to get attention
jokes.punchline: Bizarre dedication to costume authenticity
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd detail about superhero preparation, good character specificity

jokes.text: Wait a minute. There's nothing in here about the Be Sharps! Come back here! Ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about newspaper story
jokes.punchline: Newsboy scammed him with fake headline
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to previous episode, shows Homer still has ego about musical success

jokes.text: It's been done.
jokes.setup: Rooftop concert performance
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's dismissive reference to Beatles rooftop concert
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy, meta-reference to Beatles, perfectly deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Get the tear gas.
jokes.setup: Peaceful rooftop musical performance
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's violent overreaction to harmless singing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence, treating music like a riot, good character consistency

jokes.text: [Sucking] [Coughing] Your turn, Bart.
jokes.setup: Kids sharing an extremely thick Squishy drink
jokes.punchline: Physical struggle to consume the drink
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy, the struggle with thick liquid, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: What's it like, Bart? Bart? Bart? [Babbling Gibberish] Give me that!
jokes.setup: Bart trying the super thick Squishy
jokes.punchline: Bart becomes temporarily incoherent from sugar rush
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Squishy gag, Bart's extreme reaction works for his character

jokes.text: Okay. We're young, rich and full of sugar. What'll we do? Let's go crazy Broadway-style!
jokes.setup: Kids hyped up on sugar looking for activity
jokes.punchline: Deciding to go 'Broadway-style' crazy
jokes.explanation: Nice setup for musical number, kids' logic is appropriately absurd

jokes.text: New York is thataway, man. Thanks, kid.
jokes.setup: Bart and Milhouse presumably dancing toward New York
jokes.punchline: Getting actual directions to New York during their fantasy
jokes.explanation: Clever puncturing of musical fantasy with mundane reality, good absurdist touch

jokes.text: Mmm! Free goo.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering some unidentified substance
jokes.punchline: His willingness to eat anything free, regardless of what it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer behavior - free food trumps safety, excellent character consistency

jokes.text: I don't know where you magic pixies came from... but I like your pixie drink.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently hallucinating from the 'goo'
jokes.punchline: Seeing Bart and Milhouse as magic pixies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's impaired state, 'pixie drink' is a nice touch

jokes.text: [Apu Crying]
jokes.setup: Everyone claims they've found happiness
jokes.punchline: Apu clearly crying, contradicting the happy ending
jokes.explanation: Good visual contradiction of stated happiness, sets up the next revelation

jokes.text: Hey, he's not happy at all! He lied to us through song!
jokes.setup: Apu was just singing about being happy
jokes.punchline: Homer's outrage at being deceived by musical convention
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on musical theater conventions, Homer treating song lyrics as legal testimony

jokes.text: I hate when people do that!
jokes.setup: Homer's outrage at musical deception
jokes.punchline: His general hatred of this apparently common practice
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to previous joke, Homer treating this as a recurring problem

jokes.text: I really like the vest. I gathered, yeah.
jokes.setup: Burns presumably just finished an entire song about his vest
jokes.punchline: Smithers' dry acknowledgment of Burns' obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers deadpan response to Burns' narcissism, excellent character dynamic

jokes.text: Oh, I knew I should have shut that window.
jokes.setup: Presumably someone unwillingly drawn into a musical number
jokes.punchline: Treating musical infection like a draft
jokes.explanation: Clever treatment of musical numbers as contagious, like catching cold

jokes.text: [Laughter] [Chuckles] Mule.
jokes.setup: Lyle Lanley's elaborate metaphor about town with money being like a mule with a spinning wheel
jokes.punchline: Homer fixates only on the word 'mule' and finds it hilarious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer behavior - missing the point entirely and laughing at random words

jokes.text: Now wait just a minute. We're twice as smart as the people of Shelbyville. Just tell us your idea, and we'll vote for it.
jokes.setup: Lanley suggests his idea is too sophisticated for Springfield
jokes.punchline: Townsperson proves they're gullible by immediately promising to vote for an unknown idea
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - proving they're NOT smart by being immediately manipulated

jokes.text: Monorail! Mono- D'oh!
jokes.setup: Everyone chanting 'Monorail' enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Homer trips over his own enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to Homer's catchphrase, works as physical comedy punctuation

jokes.text: And now please rise for our opening hymn... uh, 'In the Garden of Eden'... by I. Ron Butterfly.
jokes.setup: Church service with traditional hymn announcement
jokes.punchline: It's actually Iron Butterfly's rock song with altered band name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pun on Iron Butterfly, perfect misdirection from religious to rock music

jokes.text: Hey, Marge, remember when we used to make out to this hymn?
jokes.setup: Rock music playing in church
jokes.punchline: Homer's nostalgic reference to romantic moments with this 'hymn'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment, Homer's obliviousness to the song's true nature

jokes.text: Wait a minute. This sounds like rock and/or roll.
jokes.setup: Iron Butterfly playing in church disguised as hymn
jokes.punchline: Ned's suspicious recognition but formal phrasing of 'rock and/or roll'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders voice - overly formal and suspicious, 'and/or' is perfectly stilted

jokes.text: Well, let's get eatin'. [Gunshots] All right, all right. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Characters about to eat after musical ends
jokes.punchline: Snake violently enforcing the end of musical numbers
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Snake, his criminal impatience with musical conventions

jokes.text: Yo! I told you to stop the music, dudes. I'm serious!
jokes.setup: End credits music continuing despite Snake's warning
jokes.punchline: Snake's increasingly frustrated attempts to end all music
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag, Snake vs. the show's musical format itself

jokes.text: Okay, I hear that. I hear that, dudes! You too, Gracie music dude!
jokes.setup: Music keeps playing despite Snake's threats
jokes.punchline: Snake acknowledging even quiet background music and addressing musicians directly
jokes.explanation: Nice meta touch, character acknowledging the show's music production, good ending gag
